sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: End of life care for adults: service delivery — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE legal, capacity, consent, and end-of-life adaptation
sources.population: Adults approaching the end of life and their carers or other important people, with consent and capacity qualifications.
sources.clinical_setting: Coordinated health and social care, including advance care planning and transitions.
sources.applicability_note: Exact only when the clinician has established that the adult is approaching end of life. Supports recording advance-care-planning participation, preferences, carers, plan availability, and review at transitions; it does not infer prognosis, capacity, goals, or treatment decisions.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.7 — advance care planning and involvement
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting that advance care planning was offered or completed, who was involved with consent, the person’s stated preferences, and where the plan is available, with capacity and legal context kept clinician-led.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.7.1 — reviewing needs, preferences, and advance plans
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-completed review of changing health and social needs, preferences, and advance plans at transitions without generating a care decision.

archive.title: End of life care for adults: service delivery — Recommendations
archive.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
archive.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE legal, capacity, consent, and end-of-life adaptation
archive.population: Adults approaching the end of life and their carers or other important people, with consent and capacity qualifications.
archive.setting: Coordinated health and social care, including advance care planning and transitions.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.7 — advance care planning and involvement
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting that advance care planning was offered or completed, who was involved with consent, the person’s stated preferences, and where the plan is available, with capacity and legal context kept clinician-led.

exact_relevant_sections.heading: Recommendation 1.7.1 — reviewing needs, preferences, and advance plans
exact_relevant_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting clinician-completed review of changing health and social needs, preferences, and advance plans at transitions without generating a care decision.

sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
sources.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.4 — multimorbidity, daily activities, falls, medicines, and frailty context
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.6.8 — treatment burden, day-to-day life, carers, goals, values, and priorities
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting functional impact, mental and physical health interaction, treatment burden, carer involvement, independence, social activity, personal goals, values, priorities, and medicine concerns.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.9–1.6.15 — medicines and treatment review
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led review of benefits, harms, treatment burden, personal priorities, and a documented discussion about reducing or stopping treatment without generating a medication change.
